mcqs.question: If X ~ Exp(λ), what is the mean of X?
mcqs.explanation: For an exponential distribution with rate parameter λ, the mean (expected value) is E(X) = 1/λ.

mcqs.question: Which property is unique to the exponential distribution among all continuous distributions?
mcqs.explanation: The exponential distribution is the only continuous distribution with the memoryless property: P(X > s+t | X > s) = P(X > t). Note: the constant hazard rate is equivalent to the memoryless property, but 'memoryless property' is the standard term used in exams.

mcqs.question: Customers arrive at a shop at an average rate of 10 per hour. What is the probability that the time between two successive arrivals is more than 12 minutes?
If X follows an exponential distribution with mean 4, what is the variance of X?
The inter-arrival times in a Poisson process with rate λ follow which distribution?
mcqs.explanation: In a Poisson process with rate λ, the time between consecutive events (inter-arrival time) follows an exponential distribution with the same rate parameter λ.
